task: Task K Gate 0 - independent Output census
headline_verdict: Design-seat structural picture holds; all DISK totals DIFFER by a 2-day snapshot drift (17->19 Jul, Task J outputs). No structural claim (registry, builder, code, Gate A) refuted. The load-bearing 431/0-registered Gate A claim and the MER/MODIS-CSV essentiality trap are CONFIRMED.

checksum_verify.method: full-file SHA-256 vs recorded checksum_sha256, all four asset tables
checksum_verify.builder_50MB_note: Builder sha256(path,max_bytes=50MB) RETURNS NULL past the cap (not a truncated hash). No non-null recorded checksum belongs to a >50MB file, so the spec's 'large rasters mismatch spuriously' trap does not arise; full-file comparison is valid for all 285.

failures.reason: regenerated .md; disk content drifted from recorded checksum (not corruption)
regenerated build report; disk content drifted from recorded checksum

workstream.note: My path/token rules classify 93.9% vs design-seat 79% (DIFFER +~15pp). Drivers: (a) all review_bundles bucketed as tier1_bundles (+305), (b) diagnostics subfolders classified by embedded MER/MODIS/background/RS_coverage tokens rather than left generic. unclassified=83 concentrated in Output/diagnostics.

essential.trap_verdict: CONFIRMED: MER/MODIS CSVs in CSV_INPUTS are load-bearing. 4 MER CSVs + review_deck MER CSV + 2 MODIS CSVs are builder inputs; retired pre/post rasters+CSVs are resolved by the path module. workstream=MER/pre_post does NOT imply essential=no.

gate_a.note: 431 files / 43 folders / 0 registry rows / 0 essential - EXACTLY as claimed. Before->after totals shift to 1351->920 (not 1326->895) due to drift; the archive-set counts themselves match precisely.
